data.text: Fourth time's the charm, and that's the one that matters most, doesn't it? Yeah, I mean, they're an unbelievable team. They're very good, and mentally for us to kind of get over that hurdle against Campbell and show up the way we did today, getting down early in the first quarter and the resolve that we had in the second quarter, we were great defensively second quarter and gave ourselves a chance in the second half, so just very proud of the girls. What was going wrong there early when you were down 14-3? We just were flustered a little bit, and we didn't settle in yet, and kind of had butterflies, I think, and we weren't making reads on our offense. We were kind of guessing on stuff, but the girls settled in and bought into what our game plan was, and we had a lot of kids step up. Defensively, again, we were great in the second quarter, but they were doubling Mesa, and we kind of recognized that, and we had kids make shots when they were open. You know, Avery shot the lights out today. Cam hit big ones. It just was a team effort. We had to have everybody we had them. You had that hot finish there the second quarter. How did you try to make sure that you didn't have the big letdown coming out of halftime? Well, we just talked at half about how we got through that quarter because we were really good defensively. We got after them a little bit, and our defense turned into offense. And we tried to carry that through the second half. And Campbell came in, and they made a lot of shots. And Cammie Curtis finishes with dang near 30, I think. But we limited everybody else. We were great on the basketball. It was our defense that carried us through the game. Obviously, we shot the ball well, but we had to get stops, and we got them. As much as nobody likes to lose, was maybe losing that regional championship game kind of a blessing in disguise considering how the state bracket was shaped and how you went through it? We had to beat really good teams to win, and we knew that regardless. So whoever we got in the first round, second round, third round, we knew we were going to have to beat good teams, and the kids stepped up and did that. considering three years ago this team only won nine games? Did he make state tournament two years ago as well and fell short in third place last year? How have you seen this program kind of turn around? Well, Addy and Allie inherited a program with two wins the year before. They come in and they work their tails off that summer and We go 9-15, but we take third at state. We win big games down the stretch. They kind of got a feel for that, but they never stopped working. Adeline and Allie came in and shot for the last four weeks, five weeks they came in and shot and made 700 shots a week in the morning. They don't know how to stop working, and they had a goal, and they continue to work towards that goal, and they deserve everything. I'm very proud of them.

data.response:
The user wants to know the jersey color of the Sheridan girls basketball team based on the provided video frames and transcript.

1.  **Analyze the Transcript:** The transcript explicitly states: "Ryan Sullivan is identified on screen as Sheridan Girls Basketball Coach. Interview statements indicate Sheridan won..." This confirms the team being interviewed is the winning team.

2.  **Analyze the Visuals:**
    *   **Frames 1-3:** These show a man in a grey plaid suit being interviewed. He is the coach mentioned in the transcript. He is not wearing a jersey.
    *   **Frames 4-6:** These show gameplay footage.
        *   In Frame 4, there are players in black jerseys and players in white jerseys. The players in black seem to be defending or rebounding.
        *   In Frame 5, a player in a white jersey is dribbling the ball down the court.
        *   In Frame 6, a player in a white jersey (number 14 visible) is shooting the ball. Another player in a white jersey (number 2 visible) is on the floor.
    *   **Connecting the dots:** The transcript says Sheridan won. The interview is with the Sheridan coach. Therefore, the Sheridan team is the winning team.
    *   **Identifying the Winning Team's Jersey:** In Frame 6, the player shooting the ball is wearing white. The player on the floor (number 2) is wearing white. The player dribbling in Frame 5 is wearing white. Usually, the team with the ball or making the play is the one scoring or driving. The coach is talking about defense carrying them, but the visual evidence of the "Sheridan" team needs to be linked to the winning status.
    *   Wait, let's look closer at the gameplay.
        *   Frame 4: A player in black is jumping for the ball. A player in white is defending.
        *   Frame 5: A player in white is dribbling fast.
